feat: initialize design reference system for high-taste capability

- Add .prism/design/ structure plan (sectors, metadata, presets)
- Extend Designskill-claude.md with library-driven taste principles
- Align with v0/bolt.new/Midjourney/Awwwards reference workflow
- Add sector buckets and curation guide for ~100 high-signal examples
- Update competition notes with design vector considerations
- Update audit-trail with recent protocol toolbar work

arguments.content:
# 03. Design Capability for Prism – Permanent Record

**Purpose of this file**  
This document serves as the canonical, unchanging reference for the high-taste design capability we are building into Prism. Because chat threads get erased and conversations fade, we leave complete plans in `.stellarumdev/fixes/` (your established practice). This file records exactly where we are going, the reasoning behind it, the planned output, and how it fits into the broader MASTER Plan III and MVP roadmap.

**Created:** 2026-05-07  
**Author:** Steven Morales (via Prism)  
**Location:** `.stellarumdev/fixes/03.design-capability.md`  
**Linked from:** `.prism/03.design-capability.md` (symlink-style reference), `00.MASTER-Plan-III.md`, `00.Current-Fixes-In-Progress.md`

---

## Vision

Transform Prism from a verification-first coding agent into a **taste-first design partner**.  
When a developer says “design a luxurious AI SaaS dashboard” or “make this landing page feel premium and 2026-native”, Prism should draw from a personally-curated, high-signal library of references (Midjourney gallery, v0 projects, Awwwards, Behance, personal critique notes) and generate UI that reflects refined aesthetic judgment rather than generic LLM tropes.

This is not just another “generate UI” feature. It is the second pillar of the Skill System:
- Skills = codified process & knowledge
- Design Library = codified taste & visual language

Together they give Prism a permanent memory of *how* we like to build software *and* *how* we like it to look.

## Why This Matters for Prism’s Competitive Position

From the competition matrix (`prism-competion-claude-discussion.md`):

- **Vector 05 – Code Generation Quality**: Currently scored 3 (“Adequate — model-agnostic by design”). The design library raises this to 5 by grounding generation in curated taste.
- **Vector 08 – Customisation & Extensibility**: Already a 5 thanks to the skill system. The design library makes taste as reusable as process.
- **Vector 03 – Context Awareness**: The library becomes rich, structured context that Skill Awareness can proactively surface.

This feature directly addresses the “fabricated aesthetic” problem we have seen with weaker models (Gemini Flash Lite inventing features or producing generic UIs). With the design library + Grounded-Chat rigor, Prism will cite real references and synthesize rather than hallucinate style.

## Library Architecture (Target Structure)

```
.stellarumdev/design/          # or .prism/design/ – we will decide final home
├── README.md                  # This document (or a summary)
├── design-core.md             # Master skill loaded by Skill Awareness
├── sectors/
│   ├── saas-dashboard.md      # Index + examples for SaaS dashboards
│   ├── marketing-landing.md
│   ├── editorial-brochure.md
│   ├── mobile-app.md
│   ├── pitch-deck.md
│   └── ...
├── images/                    # High-res reference images organized by sector
├── metadata/                  # Structured notes per reference
│   ├── saas-dashboard-01.md
│   └── ...
└── presets/
    ├── color-palettes/
    ├── typography-scales/
    ├── tailwind-config-snippets/
    └── component-patterns/
```

**Initial Sectors (Phase 0)**: saas-dashboard, marketing-landing, editorial, mobile-app, luxury-brand, neo-brutalist-tech, organic-3d, ai-tool-interface.

Target library size at launch: ~100 strong examples (10 per major sector).

## Metadata Schema (per reference)

Every entry must contain:
- `source`: midjourney | v0 | awwwards | behance | personal
- `original_prompt` (when available)
- `design_notes` / critique (why it has taste)
- `tags`: asymmetric-layout, variable-fonts, glassmorphism, micro-animations, high-contrast, neo-brutalism, etc.
- `dominant_palette`: hex values + names
- `typography`: pairings, scale, weights
- `motion_language`: subtle, energetic, cinematic, etc.
- `why_it_has_taste`: 2–4 sentence human judgment
- `sector`: primary bucket
- `visual_priority`: 1–5

## Core Skill: design-core.md

This will be the master skill. When Skill Awareness is ON, Prism will:
1. Detect design-related intent.
2. Load relevant sector indexes + 2–4 closest examples.
3. Synthesize a *new, non-derivative* aesthetic direction.
4. Always cite which library pieces influenced each decision (typography, layout rhythm, color usage, motion).
5. Generate both moodboard (vision model) and production code (React + Tailwind or whatever the project uses).

New slash command: `/design <description> [--reference <sector>]`

## Implementation Phases (Roadmap Integration)

**Phase 0 – Foundation (Current – 2026-05-07)**
- Create this permanent record (`03.design-capability.md`).
- Build initial directory skeleton.
- Write `design-core.md` skill with full taste principles.
- Populate first two sectors with ~20 references from your Midjourney gallery + v0 history.
- Update `00.MASTER-Plan-III.md` and `00.Current-Fixes-In-Progress.md` to track this as a new high-priority item under Phase 10 (Aesthetic & Feature Evolution).

**Phase 1 – Library Ingestion (Next 1–2 weeks)**
- Migrate remaining v0 designs and top Midjourney pieces.
- Curate from Awwwards, uiprompt.art, Behance.
- Extract reusable presets (color systems, typography pairings, component patterns).

**Phase 2 – Integration into Prism Core**
- Wire `design-core.md` into Skill Awareness.
- Implement `/design` command with vision model routing.
- Add taste citations to all generated UI output.

**Phase 3 – Polish & Feedback Loop**
- Tag-based similarity search.
- Weekly “Taste Digest” generation.
- Support uploading new images as references.
- Add visual consistency checks to `/audit` for design-heavy work.

**Phase 4 – Taste Evolution**
- Make the library self-improving: after each `/design` session, offer to save the output as a new reference with user critique.
- Support “evolve this design toward X” conversations that update the library.

## Success Criteria

- Generated designs visibly reference the library (citations in output).
- No more generic gradients, Inter UI, or “AI slop” aesthetics unless deliberately requested.
- User can evolve their taste over time simply by adding new examples and notes to the library.
- The system feels like an extension of *your* design sensibility (not Midjourney default or Claude’s generic taste).

## Link to Current Master Plan

This feature slots cleanly into **Phase 10 – Aesthetic & Feature Evolution** of MASTER Plan III.

It also strengthens the “Cyber-Noir” direction by giving us a systematic way to define what “premium, 2026-native, high-taste” actually means in concrete visual language.

Add to **Current Fixes In Progress**:
> 10.1 – High-taste design capability & reference library (03.design-capability.md)

We will update both `00.MASTER-Plan-III.md` and `00.Current-Fixes-In-Progress.md` after this file is written.

**Permanent Record Statement**  
This document will not be deleted. It serves as the source of truth for the design capability. All future decisions, changes, and shipped work will be appended or referenced here so we never lose the original intent.

**Next Immediate Action (after this file is saved):**  
Create the directory structure, starter metadata template, and first version of `design-core.md`.

---

**Last Updated:** 2026-05-07  
**Status:** Approved – This is the canonical plan.

STELLARUM / PRISM · SEED ROUND · 2026

Prism

The verification layer for AI-built software.

AI generates the code. Prism makes it safe to ship.

THE SHIFT

Software creation accelerated. Verification did not.

AI coding tools are increasing the volume of production code every month.

But software still ships only when someone trusts it.

The bottleneck has moved from writing code to proving it is safe to deploy.

THE PROBLEM

AI code fails silently. Not because it cannot generate code.

Because it leaves behind work that looks finished and is not:

  • incomplete refactors
  • broken callers
  • dead imports
  • drifted types
  • half-rewired routes
  • passing tests with hidden wiring gaps

The most dangerous AI bugs are the ones that appear plausible enough to merge.

THE INSIGHT

The editor should not grade its own homework.

The same agent that writes a change is structurally the wrong agent to verify it.

Today's AI coding tools optimize for:

  • generation
  • speed
  • autonomy
  • completion

Prism optimizes for:

  • verification
  • evidence
  • confidence
  • ship-readiness

Generation and verification are separate jobs.

THE SOLUTION

Prism is the second pass before production.

Run Prism after AI-generated work. It checks the change against reality:

  • compiler output
  • tests
  • symbol resolution
  • schema state
  • runtime probes
  • end-to-end flows

Prism does not ask “does this look right?” It asks “what can be proven?”

THE VALUE

Prism turns AI coding into a system teams can trust.

For developers: Ship faster with less fear.

For teams: Catch regressions earlier. Reduce review load. Keep velocity without losing confidence.

For enterprises: Create an auditable control layer for machine-generated software.

Prism increases AI adoption by reducing AI risk.

THE CONTROL POINT

Every AI-generated diff creates demand for verification.

The more code AI writes, the more verification the software stack requires.

That puts Prism at a high-value checkpoint:

between code generation and production

This is where trust is decided.

This is where budgets form.

This is where control points become companies.

THE WEDGE

Start where the pain is sharpest.

Incomplete AI refactors. This is where today's tools fail most visibly.

Prism catches:

  • missed callers
  • stale imports
  • half-rewired systems
  • broken references
  • incompatible assumptions across files

A sharp wedge wins adoption. Then expands across the full software lifecycle.

WHY PRISM WINS

Competitors built editors. We built the verification substrate.

Prism was designed around deterministic checks below the model:

  • typecheck
  • LSP diagnostics
  • tests
  • AST resolution
  • schema inspection
  • runtime probes
  • end-to-end execution

Editors can add more generation. Verification requires a different architecture.

THE PRODUCT SURFACE

One substrate. Multiple workflows.

Today:

  • /audit
  • /fix
  • /build
  • /refactor
  • /test-gen
  • /new
  • CI-friendly audit binary

Next:

  • /review-pr
  • /explain
  • enterprise policy gates
  • broader runtime verification

The wedge is verification. The platform is the trusted layer around AI software development.

THE MOAT

Trust compounds.

Prism's moat strengthens with every project:

  • persistent workspace memory
  • audit artifacts saved inside the repo
  • shared substrate across CLI, CI, and GUI
  • model-agnostic routing across 20+ models
  • project-specific knowledge that accumulates over time

The more Prism is used, the harder it is to replace.

WHY NOW

The timing window is open.

Three things changed:

  1. AI code volume is now real: AI-assisted development is moving into the mainstream.
  2. Whole-repo verification is now affordable: Long-context models and lower-cost inference make this practical.
  3. No category leader owns verification after generation: The market created the need before anyone claimed the layer.

MARKET

Prism sits inside multiple large and growing markets.

  • AI code tools: multi-billion-dollar market, growing rapidly
  • Application security and testing: multi-billion-dollar budget lines
  • Code review and CI quality gates: deeply embedded enterprise workflows

Prism sits at the intersection of:

AI coding + software quality + review + trust

If AI touches software creation, Prism has a market.

COMPETITION

Others help create code. Prism helps trust it.

CategoryExamplesPrimary job
AI editorsCursor, Copilot, Claude CodeGenerate and edit code
PR review botsCodeRabbit, BugbotReview changes near merge
Static analysisSonar, Semgrep, SnykScan for rules, security, quality
AI terminalsWarp, Cline, AiderGeneral AI-assisted development

Prism owns the verification layer after generation and before production.

PROOF

Built, working, and already validating the thesis.

  • MVP shipped
  • deterministic verification substrate live
  • IDE surface shipped
  • CI-compatible audit artifact flow live
  • 400+ tests passing across Rust + TypeScript
  • real bugs found in dogfooding
  • same substrate already powers multiple workflows

This is not a concept deck. The product exists.

BUSINESS MODEL

Verification is recurring software spend.

TierPriceBuyer
Individual$15 / monthSolo developers
Team$50 / seat / monthEngineering teams
EnterpriseCustomSecurity, platform, and dev-tooling orgs

As AI-generated code volume rises, verification spend rises with it.

VISION

Every AI-built company will need a trust layer.

The first wave of AI developer tools helped teams write faster.

The next wave will decide what is safe to ship.

Prism is building the system that answers:

  • Can we trust this change?
  • Can we prove it?
  • Is it ready for production?

Prism is the control plane for software trust in the AI era.

THE ASK

We are raising a seed round to own the verification category.

Use of funds:

  • accelerate product development
  • expand verification workflows across CI, IDE, and enterprise environments
  • win design partners and early enterprise pilots
  • establish Prism as the default second pass for AI-generated software

AI writes the code. Prism decides when it is ready.

*Prism · prism.dev · steven@dmwfinancegroup.com*

Prism was designed around deterministic checks below the model:

  • typecheck
  • LSP diagnostics
  • tests
  • AST resolution
  • schema inspection
  • runtime probes
  • end-to-end execution

Editors can add more generation. Verification requires a different architecture.

THE PRODUCT SURFACE

One substrate. Multiple workflows.

Today:

  • /audit
  • /fix
  • /build
  • /refactor
  • /test-gen
  • /new
  • CI-friendly audit binary

Next:

  • /review-pr
  • /explain
  • enterprise policy gates
  • broader runtime verification

The wedge is verification. The platform is the trusted layer around AI software development.

THE MOAT

Trust compounds.

Prism's moat strengthens with every project:

  • persistent workspace memory
  • audit artifacts saved inside the repo
  • shared substrate across CLI, CI, and GUI
  • model-agnostic routing across 20+ models
  • project-specific knowledge that accumulates over time

The more Prism is used, the harder it is to replace.

WHY NOW

The timing window is open.

Three things changed:

  1. AI code volume is now real: AI-assisted development is moving into the mainstream.
  2. Whole-repo verification is now affordable: Long-context models and lower-cost inference make this practical.
  3. No category leader owns verification after generation: The market created the need before anyone claimed the layer.

MARKET

Prism sits inside multiple large and growing markets.

  • AI code tools: multi-billion-dollar market, growing rapidly
  • Application security and testing: multi-billion-dollar budget lines
  • Code review and CI quality gates: deeply embedded enterprise workflows

Prism sits at the intersection of:

AI coding + software quality + review + trust

If AI touches software creation, Prism has a market.

COMPETITION

Others help create code. Prism helps trust it.

CategoryExamplesPrimary job
AI editorsCursor, Copilot, Claude CodeGenerate and edit code
PR review botsCodeRabbit, BugbotReview changes near merge
Static analysisSonar, Semgrep, SnykScan for rules, security, quality
AI terminalsWarp, Cline, AiderGeneral AI-assisted development

Prism owns the verification layer after generation and before production.

PROOF

Built, working, and already validating the thesis.

  • MVP shipped
  • deterministic verification substrate live
  • IDE surface shipped
  • CI-compatible audit artifact flow live
  • 400+ tests passing across Rust + TypeScript
  • real bugs found in dogfooding
  • same substrate already powers multiple workflows

This is not a concept deck. The product exists.

BUSINESS MODEL

Verification is recurring software spend.

TierPriceBuyer
Individual$15 / monthSolo developers
Team$50 / seat / monthEngineering teams
EnterpriseCustomSecurity, platform, and dev-tooling orgs

As AI-generated code volume rises, verification spend rises with it.

VISION

Every AI-built company will need a trust layer.

The first wave of AI developer tools helped teams write faster.

The next wave will decide what is safe to ship.

Prism is building the system that answers:

  • Can we trust this change?
  • Can we prove it?
  • Is it ready for production?

Prism is the control plane for software trust in the AI era.

THE ASK

We are raising a seed round to own the verification category.

Use of funds:

  • accelerate product development
  • expand verification workflows across CI, IDE, and enterprise environments
  • win design partners and early enterprise pilots
  • establish Prism as the default second pass for AI-generated software

AI writes the code. Prism decides when it is ready.

*Prism · prism.dev · steven@dmwfinancegroup.com*
arguments.old_string:
STELLARUM / PRISM · SEED ROUND · 2026

Prism

The verification layer for AI-built software.

AI generates the code. Prism makes it safe to ship.

THE SHIFT

Software creation accelerated. Verification did not.

AI coding tools are increasing the volume of production code every month.

But software still ships only when someone trusts it.

The bottleneck has moved from writing code to proving it is safe to deploy.

THE PROBLEM

AI code fails silently. Not because it cannot generate code.

Because it leaves behind work that looks finished and is not:

  • incomplete refactors
  • broken callers
  • dead imports
  • drifted types
  • half-rewired routes
  • passing tests with hidden wiring gaps

The most dangerous AI bugs are the ones that appear plausible enough to merge.

THE INSIGHT

The editor should not grade its own homework.

The same agent that writes a change is structurally the wrong agent to verify it.

Today's AI coding tools optimize for:

  • generation
  • speed
  • autonomy
  • completion

Prism optimizes for:

  • verification
  • evidence
  • confidence
  • ship-readiness

Generation and verification are separate jobs.

THE SOLUTION

Prism is the second pass before production.

Run Prism after AI-generated work. It checks the change against reality:

  • compiler output
  • tests
  • symbol resolution
  • schema state
  • runtime probes
  • end-to-end flows

Prism does not ask “does this look right?” It asks “what can be proven?”

THE VALUE

Prism turns AI coding into a system teams can trust.

For developers: Ship faster with less fear.

For teams: Catch regressions earlier. Reduce review load. Keep velocity without losing confidence.

For enterprises: Create an auditable control layer for machine-generated software.

Prism increases AI adoption by reducing AI risk.

THE CONTROL POINT

Every AI-generated diff creates demand for verification.

The more code AI writes, the more verification the software stack requires.

That puts Prism at a high-value checkpoint:

between code generation and production

This is where trust is decided.

This is where budgets form.

This is where control points become companies.

THE WEDGE

Start where the pain is sharpest.

Incomplete AI refactors. This is where today's tools fail most visibly.

Prism catches:

  • missed callers
  • stale imports
  • half-rewired systems
  • broken references
  • incompatible assumptions across files

A sharp wedge wins adoption. Then expands across the full software lifecycle.

WHY PRISM WINS

Competitors built editors. We built the verification substrate.

Prism was designed around deterministic checks below the model:

  • typecheck

  • tests
  • AST resolution
  • schema inspection
  • runtime probes
  • end-to-end execution

Editors can add more generation. Verification requires a different architecture.

  • /build
  • /refactor

  • /new
  • CI-friendly audit binary

Next:

  • broader runtime verification

MARKET
